title: Yield before a left turn
rule_summary: When turning left, yield to approaching vehicles that are close enough to be dangerous before completing the turn.

title: Yield to people in crosswalks
rule_summary: Remain stopped for pedestrians or people using mobility devices in a crosswalk until they are safely clear of your vehicle's path.

title: Highway traffic has right-of-way
rule_summary: When merging onto a highway, match traffic speed when possible and merge only when there is a safe space.

title: School bus yellow warning lights
rule_summary: Flashing yellow lights on a school bus mean slow down and prepare to stop.

title: Right turn on red
rule_summary: A right turn on red is allowed only after a complete stop and only when no sign prohibits it.

title: Stop behind the limit line
rule_summary: At a stop, stop behind the limit line; if none exists, stop before the crosswalk or before entering the intersection.

title: Basic Speed Law
rule_summary: Never drive faster than is safe for current road, traffic, visibility, and weather conditions.

title: Uncontrolled railroad crossing speed
rule_summary: When within 100 feet of an uncontrolled railroad crossing and visibility is limited to less than 400 feet in both directions, the speed limit is 15 mph.

title: Enter highways near traffic speed
rule_summary: Enter a highway at or near the speed of traffic when it is safe to do so.

title: Right turn position
rule_summary: For a right turn, drive close to the right edge or use the designated right-turn lane, signal before turning, check for vulnerable road users, and finish in the right lane.

title: Left turn position
rule_summary: For a left turn, start from the lane closest to the center divider or a left-turn lane and complete the turn when it is safe.

title: Signal and check blind spots
rule_summary: Signal, use mirrors, and quickly look over your shoulder before changing lanes or merging.

title: Parallel parking setup
rule_summary: A proper parallel parking maneuver starts by stopping next to the vehicle in front of the open space, then backing into the space.

title: Blue curb parking
rule_summary: A blue curb is for a person with a disabled person parking placard or disabled person license plate.

title: Secure the vehicle on a hill
rule_summary: When parking on a hill, set the parking brake and leave the vehicle in park or in gear.

title: Keep a safe following distance
rule_summary: Use enough following distance to react safely; increase space around large vehicles, motorcycles, poor weather, and high speeds.

title: Scan ahead and around you
rule_summary: Keep your eyes moving, scan ahead, check mirrors, and watch for hazards instead of staring at one point.

title: Use headlights when visibility is poor
rule_summary: Use headlights when weather, darkness, dust, smoke, or other conditions make it hard to see or be seen.

title: Dim high beams for oncoming traffic
rule_summary: Switch from high beams to low beams when approaching an oncoming vehicle so you do not blind the other driver.

title: Avoid handheld phone distractions
rule_summary: If you do not have a hands-free device, do not answer a ringing phone while driving.

title: Give large trucks extra space
rule_summary: Large trucks have large blind spots and need more room to stop and maneuver, so avoid following too closely.

title: Let tailgaters pass
rule_summary: If another driver is tailgating you, change lanes when safe and let the driver pass instead of braking suddenly or speeding up.

title: Roads can be slippery when rain starts
rule_summary: Roads are especially slippery during the first several minutes of rain because oil and dust have not yet washed away.

title: Opened alcohol containers must be stored lawfully
rule_summary: An opened alcoholic beverage container may be transported only where it is not accessible to the driver or passengers, such as the trunk.

title: Drugs can impair driving
rule_summary: Prescription, over-the-counter, and illegal drugs can affect judgment, coordination, and reaction time.

title: Under-21 drivers face stricter alcohol rules
rule_summary: Drivers under 21 are subject to stricter alcohol-related rules and should not drive after drinking any alcohol.

title: Report qualifying collisions with SR-1
rule_summary: A Report of Traffic Accident Occurring in California (SR-1) is required for qualifying collisions, including those involving injury or sufficient property damage.

title: Stop after a collision
rule_summary: If involved in a collision, stop, check for injuries, exchange required information, and report when required.

title: Do not stop on railroad tracks
rule_summary: Do not start across railroad tracks unless there is enough room on the other side to completely clear the tracks.

title: Flashing yellow signal
rule_summary: A flashing yellow traffic signal means slow down and proceed with caution.

title: Enter bike lane only near right turns
rule_summary: When preparing for a right turn, enter the bike lane only within the allowed distance before the turn and yield to bicyclists.

title: Solid and broken yellow lines
rule_summary: When a solid yellow line is next to a broken yellow line, passing is allowed only from the side next to the broken line when safe.

title: Red curb means no stopping or parking
rule_summary: A red curb means no stopping, standing, or parking.

title: When is it legal to drive off the road to pass another vehicle?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: It is not legal under any conditions.

title: When a railroad crossing is not controlled, what is the speed limit w...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: 15 mph.

title: Which of the following is the proper procedure for parallel parking?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Stop next to the vehicle in front of the open space, and then back into the space.

title: What speed should you be driving when entering onto a highway?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: At or near the speed of traffic.

title: How many feet away should you switch from high beam to low beam headl...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: 500 feet.

title: Who can legally park next to curb painted blue?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: A person who is disabled and has a special placard or vehicle license plate for disabled persons.

title: What should you do when there is a school bus ahead that starts flash...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Slow down and prepare to stop.

title: Which of the following is a requirement of California's Basic Speed Law?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Never drive faster than is safe for current conditions.

title: You must notify the DMV with 5 days, if you:
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Sell or transfer ownership of your vehicle.

title: Which of the following is an example of a safe driving practice?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Always keep your eyes moving to scan the surroundings.

title: What should you do when reaching an intersection where a person opera...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Remain stopped behind the crosswalk line until the motorized wheelchair has safely finished crossing.

title: Which of the following is true about large trucks?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: They have large blind spots, which makes it difficult for the truck driver to see other vehicles.

title: What is one of the most common causes of traffic collisions?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Driver distractions.

title: Any driver who willfully flees or attempts to evade law enforcement,...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Imprisonment in a state prison for up to seven years.

title: What should a driver do who is stopped at an intersection and wants t...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Give the right-of-way to any approaching vehicle that is close enough to be dangerous.

title: In addition to setting your parking brake, what should you do when pa...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Make sure your vehicle is in the "park" position or in gear.

title: You are under 18 years of age and have had your license for eight mon...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Between 5 a.m. and 11 p.m.

title: Where should you begin a left turn from a one-way street onto a one-w...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: The far-left lane.

title: You are required to notify DMV by filing a Report of Traffic Accident...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: You were involved in a collision with more than $1,000 in damages.

title: At what point during a rainfall are roads slippery on a hot day?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: For the first several minutes.

title: What color curb does not allow vehicles to stop or park?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Red.

title: You are required to notify DMV by filing a Report of Traffic Accident...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: You were involved in a collision with an injury.

title: What is indicated by two sets of double yellow lines spaced 2 feet or...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Barrier.

title: What should you do to make a right turn at an upcoming intersection?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Merge into the bicycle lane before making the turn.

title: A highway has a posted speed limit of 65 mph. What does this mean?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: You must drive 65 mph only if driving conditions are ideal.

title: When is parking in a crosshatched (diagonal lines) area allowed?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: It is never allowed to park in a crosshatched (diagonal lines) area.

title: What should you do if your cell phone rings and you do not have a han...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Do not answer the phone and let it go to voice mail.

title: If a traffic signal is green, but traffic is blocking the intersectio...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Wait and do not enter the intersection until your vehicle can get completely across.

title: In which lane should you end your turn when making a right turn?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: The lane closest to the right edge of the road.

title: When is it required for you to obey directions from a crossing guard?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: At all times.

title: Which of the following is true about large trucks?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: They have large blind spots, which makes it difficult for the truck driver to see other vehicles.

title: Which of the following is illegal while driving?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Wearing a headset or ear plugs that covers both ears.

title: What should you do when traffic is slow and heavy, and you must cross...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Wait until you can completely cross the tracks before proceeding.

title: Which of the following should you do if you are being followed by a t...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Change lanes and allow the tailgater to pass.

title: When you are driving the speed limit, you can be given a speeding tic...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: If road or weather conditions require a slower speed.

title: Which lights should be used if a driver is having trouble seeing othe...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Headlights.

title: What should you do to make a right turn at an upcoming intersection?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Merge into the bicycle lane before making the turn.

title: What does a flashing yellow traffic signal light direct a driver to do?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Slow down and proceed with caution.

title: What should you do if a pedestrian is still in the crosswalk after th...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Wait until the pedestrian has crossed the street completely before proceeding.

title: When is it legal for a person to drive with an alcoholic beverage con...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: If the container is in the trunk of the vehicle.

title: You are driving on the freeway. The vehicle in front of you is a larg...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Farther behind the truck than you would for a passenger vehicle.

title: All of the following practices are dangerous to do while driving. Whi...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Listening to music through headphones that cover both ears.

title: Always stop before you cross railroad tracks when:
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: You don't have room on the other side to completely cross the tracks.

title: When you tailgate other drivers (drive close to their rear bumper):
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: You can frustrate the other drivers and make them angry.

title: Should you always drive slower than other traffic?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: No, you can block traffic when you drive too slowly.

title: You see a signal person at a road construction site ahead. You should...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: At all times.

title: When can you drive in a bike lane?
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: When you are within 200 feet of a cross street where you plan to turn right.

title: You see a flashing yellow traffic signal at an upcoming intersection....
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Slow down and cross the intersection carefully.

title: There is no crosswalk and you see a pedestrian crossing your lane ahe...
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Stop and let him/her finish crossing the street.

title: A solid yellow line next to a broken yellow line means that vehicles:
rule_summary: Official DMV sample test item. Correct answer: Next to the broken line may pass.
